name: Http.Retry

unions.name: FailureCondition
unions.comment: FailureCondition contains a function that filter request errors

values.name: onAllFailures
values.comment: To use with `with` function. Filters errors to be retried by retrying all errors

values.name: onNetworkError
values.comment: To use with `with` function. Filters errors to be retried by retrying all network errors

values.name: onStatus
values.comment: To use with `with` function. Filters errors to be retried by retrying errors based on the status code

values.name: onTimeout
values.comment: To use with `with` function. Filters errors to be retried by retrying all timeout errors

values.name: onUnauthenticatedStatus
values.comment: To use with `with` function. Filters errors to be retried by retrying errors base on the 401 status code

values.name: onUnauthorizedStatus
values.comment: To use with `with` function. Filters errors to be retried by retrying errors base on the 403 status code

values.name: with
values.comment:
Given a list of error handling `Policy` from [`Retry` package](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/choonkeat/elm-retry/latest/) we can make our `originalTask`
retry on failure until any one of the `Policy` fails.

    originalTask
        |> Http.Retry.with
            [ Retry.maxDuration 7000
            , Retry.exponentialBackoff { interval = 500, maxInterval = 3000 }
            ]
            [ Http.Retry.onUnauthenticatedStatus ]
        |> Task.perform DidOriginalTask
values.type: List.List (Retry.Policy Http.Error.RequestError) -> List.List Http.Retry.FailureCondition -> Task.Task Basics.Never (RemoteData.RemoteData Http.Error.RequestError data) -> Task.Task Basics.Never (RemoteData.RemoteData Http.Error.RequestError data)
